stem: A healthcare operations study measures the wait time (minutes) for a sample of 25 emergency room visitors. The sample mean is 291.4 min with a sample standard deviation of 71.5 min. However, 3 of the 25 observations appear to be outliers (values more than 2.5 standard deviations from the mean). If these outliers are removed, the remaining 22 observations have a mean of 281.6 min and standard deviation of 48.9 min. Estimate the true population mean and provide a 95% confidence interval that accounts for the uncertainty about whether outliers should be included.

ground_truth.answer: With all data: mean = 291.4, 95% CI = [261.9, 320.9]. Without outliers: mean = 281.6, 95% CI = [260.7, 302.5]. Combined interval accounting for analytic uncertainty: [260.7, 320.9]. Point estimate: 286.5 min.

solution_steps: The outlier treatment choice creates structural uncertainty. Combined interval: [260.7, 320.9].
common_failure_modes: Using only the full sample without considering outlier impact
Removing outliers without reporting both analyses
Not recognizing that the choice of analysis method is itself a source of uncertainty
